Scenario: Valid local login
Description: Submit valid email/password to sign-in API and verify success response.
Test Steps: 1. Send POST /api/users/sign_in with valid user credentials. 2. Assert success flag and user object. 3. Capture token/session artifacts.
Expected Result: HTTP 200 with success=true and expected user metadata.

Scenario: Invalid password rejection
Description: Verify incorrect password is rejected without disclosing sensitive details.
Test Steps: 1. Send POST sign-in with valid email and wrong password. 2. Validate failure response payload.
Expected Result: Authentication fails with safe error message and no privileged session.

Scenario: Unknown user rejection
Description: Ensure unknown account cannot authenticate.
Test Steps: 1. Send POST sign-in with non-existent email. 2. Validate failure contract.
Expected Result: Authentication fails with non-enumerative error handling.

Scenario: Missing email validation
Description: Validate missing email field handling.
Test Steps: 1. Send sign-in payload without user.email. 2. Verify error response.
Expected Result: Request is rejected with validation error, no session created.

Scenario: Missing password validation
Description: Validate missing password field handling.
Test Steps: 1. Send sign-in payload without user.password. 2. Verify error response.
Expected Result: Request is rejected with validation error, no session created.

Scenario: Email case normalization
Description: Validate login works with case variations in email.
Test Steps: 1. Submit same account in upper/lower case email forms. 2. Compare results.
Expected Result: Authentication behavior is case-insensitive and consistent.

Scenario: Whitespace trimming
Description: Validate leading/trailing spaces in login input are handled safely.
Test Steps: 1. Send email/password with leading/trailing spaces. 2. Verify behavior.
Expected Result: Input sanitization is predictable and secure.

Scenario: Disabled user blocked
Description: Ensure disabled users cannot authenticate.
Test Steps: 1. Mark user disabled. 2. Attempt login. 3. Validate denial.
Expected Result: Authentication denied for disabled account.

Scenario: Role claim consistency
Description: Validate returned user role is accurate and not mutable by request.
Test Steps: 1. Login with role-specific user. 2. Compare response role with DB/system role.
Expected Result: Returned role matches authoritative role mapping.

Scenario: Token header auth path
Description: Validate token-based header auth when authentication_token exists.
Test Steps: 1. Login and extract token. 2. Call protected endpoint with X-User-Email/X-User-Token.
Expected Result: Protected call succeeds only with valid header pair.

Scenario: Token tampering rejection
Description: Ensure modified token is rejected.
Test Steps: 1. Alter token value. 2. Call protected endpoint.
Expected Result: Unauthorized result without data exposure.

Scenario: Session invalidation on logout
Description: Validate logout invalidates active session/token.
Test Steps: 1. Login then sign out. 2. Retry protected endpoint using old token/session.
Expected Result: Post-logout access is denied.

Scenario: Concurrent login handling
Description: Validate behavior for multi-session login from same account.
Test Steps: 1. Login from two clients. 2. Call protected APIs from both.
Expected Result: Session policy is enforced consistently.

Scenario: Brute-force guard
Description: Assess lockout/rate-limiting on repeated failures.
Test Steps: 1. Attempt repeated invalid logins. 2. Measure response and lockout behavior.
Expected Result: Protection controls activate without service instability.

Scenario: Credential stuffing pattern resilience
Description: Validate service response under password-spray style attempts.
Test Steps: 1. Attempt sequential invalid credentials on many users. 2. Monitor responses.
Expected Result: No user enumeration; security controls and monitoring behave as designed.

Scenario: Session fixation resistance
Description: Check session identifiers rotate appropriately after auth.
Test Steps: 1. Capture pre-login session cookie. 2. Login. 3. Compare session identity.
Expected Result: Session fixation is prevented.

Scenario: Error contract consistency
Description: Ensure all auth failures follow consistent schema.
Test Steps: 1. Trigger multiple auth failure modes. 2. Compare response contracts.
Expected Result: Failure payload shape remains consistent across auth errors.

Scenario: Sensitive field redaction
Description: Ensure auth responses do not leak sensitive internals.
Test Steps: 1. Run auth success/failure paths. 2. Inspect payload and logs.
Expected Result: No password/hash/internal secret leakage.

Scenario: Replay of previous auth artifacts
Description: Verify stale/replayed auth artifacts are denied.
Test Steps: 1. Reuse old token/session artifact. 2. Call protected endpoint.
Expected Result: Replay attempts are blocked as per policy.

Scenario: Auth audit observability
Description: Validate audit trail is generated for sign-in outcomes.
Test Steps: 1. Trigger success and failure logins. 2. Verify audit/event records.
Expected Result: Auth events are traceable with actionable metadata.
